title: RUSTSEC-2023-0071: rsa crate Marvin Attack (no upstream fix)
description: The rsa crate v0.9.10 has a known timing side-channel in PKCS#1 v1.5 decryption (Marvin Attack, CVSS 5.9). No fixed version is available. We only use rsa for SIGNING (not decryption), so the attack surface is limited to signature verification. The signing path uses SigningKey which is not affected. The verification path uses VerifyingKey which is potentially affected but the attacker would need to observe many verification operations with chosen ciphertexts to exploit it. Mitigation: document as accepted risk. Monitor for upstream fix. Consider switching to ed25519 when ActivityPub ecosystem supports it.
close_reason: Cargo audit: rsa/instant accepted as known risk. Fuzz: ammonia panic fixed, 3 fuzz targets created. Ops: file permission warning at startup, quality gates in CLAUDE.md, signing error sanitized in last_error.
